title: Access Control Panel Tamper and Line Fault
dataSources: Ingest controller diagnostics into `index=physical` as `sourcetype="access_control:panel"` with Splunk-extracted numeric flags **`tamper`**, **`line_fault`**, and enumerated **`power_state`**, plus dimensions **`panel_id`** and **`fault_type`** that your normalisation maps from the vendor feed. Land syslog or structured UDP/TCP from the access head-end through **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase app **4740**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)) on a Heavy Forwarder, or forward compact JSON through **HTTP Event Collector** `POST /services/collector/event`, aligning timestamps and FIELDALIAS rules with the **Access panel diagnostics** normalisation app referenced for this stream.
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="access_control:panel"
| where tamper=1 OR line_fault=1 OR power_state!="normal"
| stats count by panel_id, fault_type
| sort -count
description: Supervised access-control field panels report enclosure tamper loops, RS-485 bus or cable-to-ground trouble, and loss of healthy DC power supervision; this use case surfaces those hardware-level fault codes before the door electronics can be bypassed or left unsupervised.
value: We help colocation and enterprise data center teams catch physical access-layer compromise early: when a panel signals tamper or line-level electrical faults, operators can dispatch security, preserve video and badging evidence, and coordinate with facilities before an adversary completes a forced entry or silent override.
implementation: Schedule a high-severity alert when `tamper=1`, sustained `line_fault=1`, or `power_state` leaves the healthy baseline; page physical security and facilities, capture panel and door IDs for chain-of-custody, and mute only inside approved change windows that tag the affected `panel_id`.
visualization: Primary **splunk.table** of `panel_id`, `fault_type`, `power_state`, `count`, and last `_time`; companion **splunk.timeline** filtered to `tamper=1`; **splunk.singlevalue** for distinct compromised panels in the chosen window with drilldown to the underlying events in `index=physical`.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Optional: if you FIELDALIAS panel actor context into the Authentication data model for reader correlation, accelerate only after cardinality review on sparse tamper volumes; most teams keep this search on raw `index=physical` with a fifteen-minute summary snapshot instead.

references.title: Splunk Lantern â€” operations use-case library
Splunk Docs â€” Get data from TCP and UDP ports
Splunk Docs â€” HTTP Event Collector token examples

knownFalsePositives:
â€˘ **Head-end firmware or database rebuild window** â€” the **access management server** may pulse `power_state` while panels stay secure; suppress with a lookup of maintenance windows keyed on `panel_id` or mute when simultaneous `tamper=0` across the site.
â€˘ **Contractor RS-485 re-termination** during cage work can spike `line_fault` on a single bus; distinguish by pairing events with CMDB **work_order** tags or requiring **three consecutive** faulted buckets before paging.
â€˘ **DC plant battery load tests** announce `power_state=on_battery` without tamper; treat as benign when **`tamper=0`** and the message token matches your documented UPS test macro, or when facilities posts a scheduled test in the shared calendar index.
â€˘ **Spurious grounding after nearby electrical work** may raise transient `line_fault` on neighboring panels; correlate with **`dc(panel_id)`** cardinalityâ€”if more than six unrelated panels spike in one minute, escalate to facilities before assuming physical attack.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads plus an ingestion tier approved by physical security; prefer **Splunk Connect for Syslog** (Splunkbase **4740**) or an equivalent Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` TCP/UDP listener, or route vendor JSON through HEC (`/services/collector/event`) with TLS and token rotation per docs/implementation-guide.md.
â€˘ Deploy or verify the **Access panel diagnostics** normalization package so events arrive as `sourcetype="access_control:panel"` on `index=physical` with `tamper`, `line_fault`, `power_state`, `panel_id`, and `fault_type` extracted in `props.conf` / transformsâ€”spot-check `_raw` vs extracted fields in Search before alerting.
â€˘ RBAC: grant a `physical_security_analyst` role search rights on `index=physical`, `schedule_search`, and `edit_alert_actions` only for the service account that owns this correlation; map Splunk Cloud patterns to `sc_admin`-approved equivalents.

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
â€˘ Confirm the head-end exports tamper, RS-485 supervision, and DC feed alarms without aggregation delay beyond sixty seconds; document VLAN, syslog priority, and any passive TAP requirement in the facility runbook.
â€˘ Set retention hot/warm policies long enough to investigate tamper chains (commonly ninety days) while honoring privacy guidance on location fields.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
â€˘ Paste the packaged SPL (unchanged) into Save As Alert, set `dispatch.earliest_time=-5m` with `cron_schedule=*/5 * * * *`, throttle on `panel_id`, and require two consecutive fault buckets before paging line_fault-only noise.
â€˘ Add workflow actions linking to your access ticketing queue and on-site camera layout maps.

Step 3 â€” Validate
â€˘ Use a lab panel or maintenance sandbox to toggle the tamper switch under supervision and verify Splunk shows `tamper=1` with the correct `panel_id`; repeat with a controlled RS-485 disconnect if your vendor permits.
â€˘ Compare counts against the OEM head-end alarm console for the same fifteen-minute window.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize and troubleshoot
â€˘ Dashboard: table of `panel_id`, `fault_type`, last event; timeline of `tamper`; single-value for distinct panels breached in twenty-four hours.
â€˘ If storms or power transfer events correlate with mass `line_fault`, coordinate with facilities before muting; never silence `tamper=1` without human approval.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a labelled test enclosure, latch the supervised tamper circuit into the faulted position (or replay a sandbox JSON event) so Splunk ingests `index=physical` `sourcetype="access_control:panel"` rows with **`tamper=1`**, **`panel_id=LAB-PNL-01`**, and non-empty **`fault_type`** within a five-minute window; the saved alert should fire exactly once after throttling rules and include the panel identifier in the notification payload.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Replay steady-state **`access_control:panel`** maintenance heartbeats where **`tamper=0`**, **`line_fault=0`**, and **`power_state="normal"`** for **`panel_id=LAB-PNL-01`** throughout two contiguous five-minute cron windows; the scheduled search must return zero qualifying rows because none of the SPL predicates (`tamper=1`, `line_fault=1`, `power_state!="normal"`) evaluate true, proving the detector idle path.

evidence: Export the saved search â€śAccess panel tamper and line fault correlationâ€ť results (or the executive single-value panel) weekly to the physical security evidence share, retain the alert firing history table with `panel_id` and acknowledgement user, and snapshot the dashboard PNG for auditor walkthroughs.
exclusions: Does not replace the manufacturerâ€™s authoritative alarm console, certify wiring, or assess logical identity stores; badge-only denials without panel hardware faults, pure video analytics, or mantrap mechanical interlocks that never emit `access_control:panel` telemetry are out of scope unless translated into this sourcetype.
equipmentModels: RS-485 supervised access door panel (tamper and DC supervision)
